review_title: I Would Only Choose Earth Class Mail
description: I don't want any of my business mail coming to my home address.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I would only choose this software for my virtual mailbox.
cons: Cancelling and the various month to month charge to my account that normally doesn't stay the same but stays in the same range.

review_title: Twelve year user of Earth Class Mail
author: John B.
pros: A broad selection of addresses, fast and convenient document scanning, quick check deposits, simple shipping, and package management.
cons: The user interface feels ten years old. Services are priced at a premium and while the product is very good it falls slightly short of the price point. A more user-friendly app and the website would bring the pricing into line with my expectations.

review_title: Check Theft
description: The most logical point of theft of a check is the mail receiving /forwarding employees but EC never explained what what their process is, if they use vendors, who those vendors were or any other material information in connection with this criminal report I had to file at the Atlanta PD. If your use-case for a virtual office is to get an mailing address, EC would be ok but there are much cheaper vendors that do the same thing. If you need to receive customer checks (my businesses' customers are enterprise-level companies that do not pay vendors by ACH), then do not use this service.
author: John S.
pros: Ease of obtaining a physical mailing address for business purposes but that's about it.
cons: Complete refusal to assist and provide information when alledgedly an affiliate (presumably in their mail receiving/forwarding division) stole a $18250 check payable to my business.

review_title: Great product for our fully remote company
description: As a fully remote company with no physical office, Earth Class Mail has been a great solution for all of our mail receiving needs, especially for our human resource and accounting teams.
author: Sarah K.
pros: Earth Class Mail is a great solution for receiving mail with no physical offices and the ability to see scans, assign recipients, and receive physical mail (if needed) have been pros along the way.
cons: The Earth Class Mail user interface can be a little confusing, and the notifications are not as customizable as we would like them to be.

review_title: Great solution to manage the mess of mail (paper) that a small business creates
description: We use ECM extensively to manage the mail that we get running several small consulting and software resale companies. Before ECM getting all of the right paperwork to the right place was a pain. After moving online can now manage our mail from wherever we are. Unlike 'virtual office' packages that we've looked at ECM is focused on one thing: mail. They do a pretty darn good job of it too. No worrying about missing letters, you get simple controls to delete the junk mail and fast scanning on the important stuff. Even when we've had problems customer support is great. We've learned some limitations (don't get a laptop shipped to your ECM address if you need them to forward it on via next day shipment, lithium ion shipping rules apply to them as well) but even when we ran into them it was easy to get ahold of support and they did the best they could to sort the problem quickly and efficiently. Good people, great product. Best solution out there for small companies looking to unburden themselves of paper mail.
author: Adam P.
pros: It is easy to use and has lots of control. Build mail routing rules, scanning rules, setup check deposit, etc.
cons: The platform is a little older, not as many modern design elements, but it gets the job done and the functionality is great, and on some level I don't need every site I use to look like Facebook.

review_title: Bad for receiving checks
author: Roger G.
pros: I do not like their services.
cons: They take very long to process mail. This is bad for cashflow.

review_title: Run by truly awful people
description: I've used literally probably hundreds of software providers, these people are genuinely the Spirt air of vendors.
author: James W.
pros: 80% of the time they're pretty good about quickly scanning things.
cons: Run by the scum of the earth. The most predatory operators i've ever seen and I've used a lot of software. They have built numerous dark patterns into the product to make it difficult to see what you will be charged for and they've doubled pricing in the last year. There are alternatives that have much better reviews for literally 1/3 of the cost

review_title: Hassel Free
description: All my mail is received by EarthClass, they scan it for me, and I have the dhoose to Keep the hard copy, or Shred it
author: Juan D.
pros: The Portal online makes it easy to navigate like if It was Email.
cons: Pretty much cant be easier. They do all the work for you, Scan, and if you sign up , they can even deposit checks to your banking account if needed.

review_title: Great product
author: Lindsay G.
pros: It really works perfectly for my mobile business. I can work everywhere in the world and not worry about my mail. I can access it everywhere! It allows me to send checks as well if I need to. I love it.
cons: It is a bit expensive since I do not use it a ton for the price.

review_title: Unbelievably bad customer service; cost me time, energy, and money
description: I have never dealt with a more incompetent company in my entire life. Every single customer service issue is handled in the worst possible way, they question anything you do, and they are the absolute masters of surprise billing. Avoid, avoid avoid!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's tough to think of anything; it was just very poor all around.
cons: Customer service was the most incompetent I've experienced across 30 years of business purchasing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Run away
description: Such an odd bunch they are. You tell them you want to cancel, complete the official cancellation request and then they charge you anyway. When you request a refund based on the emailed cancellation conversation, they decline and instead forward you a screenshot of an email from 15 days earlier where we signed up for the service. Perhaps they don't understand time moves forward in a linear direction? I understand I signed up at one point, I then cancelled (because who wants to wait 2-3 weeks to find out they have mail?) Anyway, save yourself the trouble and stress and find another mail forwarding company.
author: John G.
pros: I like how it's all digital and they have a nice interface. Besides that, nothing.
cons: You have to wait 2-3 weeks for your mail. They charge you after cancelling. Customer service will argue with you. They do not understand the space
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: I had a wonderful experience with Earth Class Mail
description: I enjoy the product. Will recommend it to others..
author: Makedah B.
pros: I love the online Notary they provided so that the paperwork was able to be submitted in a timely manner. I know the Notary was a third party service, but it was cool how we were able to get all the paperwork completed online. I love their Register Agent product as well..
cons: I have to think hard about the cons.. Can't think of anything at the moment. Okay, I thought of something, I didn't see their phone number on the site. I think it'll be best to list it on the site for the customers who need to make a quick call can do so.. That's about all I could think of at the moment.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Generally good; wish there were an updated interface with better UX, better mobile app
author: Ashe D.
pros: Easy to manage the recycling, scanning, and shipping of my mail from anywhere, which is nice because I travel often for work.
cons: Hard to figure out how to do things beyond the most obvious things, like creating rules for certain kinds of mail

review_title: I've never had an issue with Earth Class mail.
author: Ryan S.
pros: What I like most about Earth Class mail is that I can read and manage my mail from anywhere in the world.
cons: What I like least about Earth Class mail is that it's a little pricy.

review_title: ECM ... lifesaver since I travel a lot. Fairly easy to use and customer support ... very helpful.
author: Carina K.
pros: I can view my inbox and preview the mail at the same time. I like the desktop version better than mobile.
cons: I always get charged storage fees that I can't seem to track easily. I cannot go back to where I left off. I have to start over at home page all the time.

review_title: It has been a seamless experience with no hassles so far. I appreciate timely help and care.
author: Alper T.
pros: The ease of set up. The process flow that was drawn had most of all cases covered and that tells me that the company is really caring towards its customers. I do recommend it to everyone that is in need of a similar service. Even the transfer of shipping to a centralized operation location has been seamless.
cons: The only thing I would improve is to get the non-responsive website to become mobile responsive because in this day and age it looks pretty out dated.

review_title: Excellent Mail services
description: Business presence in target market, with mail forwarding service
author: Charles B.
pros: Wonderful Business presence in multiple cities. Quick mail scan and forwarding options. Fastest customer service I have seen to date.
cons: Prices to forward mail from Oregon to California seem high and it always takes a little longer than expected (approx 4-5 days).

review_title: EarthClass Mail has streamlined our administration needs
description: We have used EarthClass Mail for 5+ years and couldn't be happier. Our mail is scanned and our bank deposits are made quickly and efficiently. Having our mail received by ECM frees up time to work on other more pressing items and it has helped keep our office running paper free.
author: Sandy H.

review_title: Great simple product, and getting better all the time
author: Bettina M.
pros: I'm a big fan of this product. It's made processing payments for our small company so much easier. No more trips to the post office, no more delays in getting payments posted. We are very pleased.
cons: There are a few functions that can be clunky, however, I will say that Earthclass is terrific at taking and implementing suggestions as they continue to develop. So I have no complaints.

review_title: Glowing review for Earth Class Mail
description: Our overall experience has been excellent. The support staff is very user friendly and quick to respond. The software itself is very easy to use and navigate.
author: Melissa H.
pros: Earth Class Mail has been a lifesaver during this pandemic. Without it the company would have lost critical mail and time in responding to necessary correspondence. Since the work from home status has been put into place, we would not be able to function without Earth Class Mail
cons: Not applicable at this time. We currently have nothing negative to say about Earth Class Mail, as we only have positive things to say

review_title: Don't Leave Home Without It!
description: Having lived abroad now for going on 8 years, I just don't know where I'd be without the ECM tentacle to my life back in the States. They forward my favorite products that I can't get overseas and handle the occasional check deposit. Thanks ECM for turning problematic into 'simplematic' for this expat!
author: Barnaby Z.
pros: User friendly menu guided "step by step" system.
cons: No longer allows me to deposit funds to my ECM account without customer service assistance. Also, shouldn't need customer service to arrange the occasional check deposit. More shipping method options would be nice, but not critical.

review_title: I could not operate my business without it.
description: Don't have to waste time with stupid mail.
author: Thomas N.
pros: The fact that I don't have to handle mail. I can be out of the country for 6 months and still see all my mail. Every piece of mail I open is automatically scanned and digitally archived. I think snail mail is one of the most inefficient things on earth, so I'm glad that ECM exists to help me streamline that part of my life.
cons: About the software - sometimes it's a little slow to load, especially if I'm scrolling way way back through my mail. I wish there wasn't such a delay in the time it takes to get my mail (I use the Las Vegas PO box and I understand that once it is received there, it's shipped up to Oregon for sorting). Sometimes giving out my address is difficult. Some automated web forms don't have room or won't accept that "PMB 61891" part of the address. I wish there was a Nevada street address.

review_title: I have had multiple accounts over the years, and currently I am an administrator for three addresses
author: Noah G.
pros: The basic premise of the service has been met, which is important for the price point of ECM. I have seen ECM change over the past few years, and I am glad to see the pricing match the service level. There is no substitute for performance.
cons: I have access to multiple accounts for various clients as well as myself. Quickbooks Online provides a great method for a master account to manage multiple accounts, and it would be beneficial if there was such a functionality for ECM.

review_title: Great idea very unreliable execution
description: I love the potential of this business if they could only execute.
author: Paul F.
pros: It has lots of excellent features. It is super great for mail processing and forwarding.
cons: The human team executing the operation is extremely unreliable. They shut down frequently, for extended times, and without notice. They have left me in the lurch on several occasions.

review_title: Best Mail Scanning Software
author: Vivien K.
pros: I really love how easily we are able to scan mail. It really helped with managing our mail that comes in physically. It takes away the need of going through the mail ourselves
cons: It becomes a hassle when we have physical packages or checks that gets delivered to our virtual mailbox by accident. The cost can pile up very quick, so we always have to be careful of sending physical packages to our virtual mail address. They charge a fee to send each piece of mail to you. Getting onboarded with the software was a very challenging as it was very complicated and confusing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
